title: Alert
description: The plaintext payload of an alert, produced on the child device by the on-device AI and enforcement layers. This object is end-to-end encrypted before it leaves the device (see packages/pairing-protocol). INVARIANT: this schema carries only structured metadata and an optional short, length-capped snippet. It MUST NOT carry raw media, full message bodies, file paths to media, base64 content, or URLs that point to a child's content. additionalProperties is false everywhere to enforce this.

v.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

id.description: Unique alert id (UUID v4), generated on the child device.

childDeviceId.description: Opaque device id assigned at pairing. Not a phone number, IMEI, or other hardware identifier.

severity.description: Two-tier model plus an info level for non-content events. 'severe' is the high-priority tier; 'all' is the moderate tier (mirrors Bark's sensitivity model); 'info' is for filter/limit/tamper events.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp from the child device clock.

source.description: Where the event came from, as an app or surface label (for example 'sms', 'instagram', 'chrome', 'photos', 'system'). Not the content itself.

modality.description: What kind of signal produced the alert.

rationale.description: Optional short, human-readable reason from the on-device classifier (for example 'language indicating self-harm'). Not the analyzed content.

snippet.description: Optional short context snippet for TEXT modality only. This is the ONLY field that may contain any portion of a child's content, it is capped at 280 characters, it is never the full message, and the parent chooses whether to view it. MUST be omitted for image modality (we never include any portion of a flagged image).

$comment: Forbidden by design (do not add these fields, ever): rawText, body, fullMessage, image, imageData, base64, mediaPath, filePath, mediaUrl, contentUrl, attachment, thumbnail, screenshot. CI tests assert these keys never appear. Adding any of them would turn an alert relay into a content store and cross the CSAM/wiretap/COPPA lines.
